# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any

    parameters {
        string(name: 'IMAGE_NAME', defaultValue: 'my-app', description: 'Name of the Docker image (e.g., my-app)')
        string(name: 'IMAGE_TAG', defaultValue: 'latest', description: 'Tag for the Docker image (e.g., v1.0.0)')
        string(name: 'REGISTRY_URL', defaultValue: '', description: 'Full URL of the Docker registry (e.g., myregistry.example.com)')
    }

    stages {
        stage('Build and Push Docker Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def fullImageName = "${params.IMAGE_NAME}:${params.IMAGE_TAG}"
                    def fullRegistryImageName = params.REGISTRY_URL ? "${params.REGISTRY_URL}/${fullImageName}" : fullImageName

                    echo "Building Docker image: ${fullImageName}"
                    sh "docker build -t ${fullImageName} ."

                    if (params.REGISTRY_URL) {
                        echo "Pushing Docker image to registry: ${fullRegistryImageName}"
                        sh "docker push ${fullRegistryImageName}"
                    } else {
                        echo "REGISTRY_URL not provided. Skipping Docker push to an external registry."
                        echo "Image ${fullImageName} built locally."
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
